name: Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla - circular by Alcalá la Real
name_original: Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla - circular por Alcalá la Real
description: This 15.0 miles (24.2 km) touring cycling route takes you through olive groves and to the historic Fortaleza de la Mota near Alcalá la Real.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcalá La Real offers diverse landscapes characterized by extensive olive groves, historic fortresses, and surrounding mountain ranges. The region features varied terrain, from lower elevations suitable for gentle rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs into the hills. This area, located in the province of Jaén, Spain, provides a blend of cultural heritage and natural beauty for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alcalá La Real The most popular touring cycling route is Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla - circular by Alcalá la Real, a 15.0 miles (24.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This route explores the area's olive groves and leads to the historic Fortaleza de la Mota. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Castillo de Locubín – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real, a difficult 31.7 miles (51.1 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes with notable elevation changes, offering views of the surrounding countryside. Local touring cyclists also love the Bike loop from Alcalá la Real, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail leading through local olive groves and villages, often completed in about 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcalá La Real is defined by vast olive groves, historic fortresses like the Fortaleza de la Mota, and the surrounding mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation gain. The routes in Alcalá La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcalá La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of touring cycling routes can I find around Alcalá La Real?
A: Alcalá La Real offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes, from easy loops through olive groves to more challenging rides with significant elevation gain into the surrounding mountains. You'll find routes suitable for various fitness levels, exploring both the agricultural landscape and historic sites.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for an easier pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 8.2 km (5.1 miles) trail that takes about 39 minutes, leading through local olive groves and villages. This type of route is generally well-suited for families or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Castillo de Locubín – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes over 51.1 km (31.7 miles). Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38250861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dornajuelo Spring – Alcalá la Real loop from Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.5 km (20.2 miles) with substantial climbing, sometimes involving unpaved segments requiring good fitness.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while cycling around Alcalá La Real?
A: The region is dominated by extensive olive groves, often referred to as a 'sea of olives'. You can also encounter unique natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocky Trail Through Gorge and Olive Groves&lt;/a&gt;, the sharply cut rocks of Los Tajos, and the vast, stony tableland of Los Llanos. The surrounding mountain ranges, such as Sierra de la Martina, offer impressive views and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by touring bike?
A: Absolutely. A prominent historical site is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota (Castle of Alcalá la Real)&lt;/a&gt;, perched on a hill, offering panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995536127/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-charilla-circular-by-alcala-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla - circular by Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; route specifically leads to this historic fortress. You might also encounter other castles in the wider area, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Alcalá La Real?
A: The region generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are typically mild, making cycling possible, though some higher elevation routes might be cooler.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995536127/fortaleza-de-la-mota-y-charilla-circular-by-alcala-la-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla - circular by Alcalá la Real&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route. The broader Senda Ciclable de las Aldeas (Villages Cycling Path) is also a significant circular route in the region, exploring diverse landscapes and rural communities.

Q: Can I find bike repair services in Alcalá La Real or nearby towns?
A: Yes, Alcalá La Real, as a regional center, should have facilities for bike repair. For specific services, it's advisable to check local listings or inquire upon arrival. Smaller towns like Castillo de Locubín, which is often part of cycling routes, may also offer basic repair options.

Q: Are there any waterfalls I can visit on a touring cycling trip?
A: While not directly on every touring route, the wider area around Alcalá La Real features several waterfalls that can be incorporated into a trip or visited separately. Notable examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chircales Sanctuary and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4225063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zurreón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3155607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Caballo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. These are often found in more rugged, natural settings.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alcalá La Real?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alcalá La Real are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 180 touring cyclists have explored the varied terrain using komoot, often praising the scenic olive groves and the blend of cultural and natural beauty.

Q: Is there public transport access to the cycling routes, or is parking available?
A: Alcalá La Real is a town with bus connections to larger cities, which might allow for transport of bikes, though this should be confirmed with specific operators. For those arriving by car, parking is generally available within the town, serving as a convenient starting point for many routes. Specific trailheads might have limited parking, so planning ahead is recommended.

Q: Are there any long-distance cycling paths nearby, like a 'Greenway'?
A: While the famous &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%ADa_verde_del_Aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Aceite (Greenway of Oil)&lt;/a&gt; does not pass directly through Alcalá La Real, it is accessible from nearby towns such as Alcaudete and Martos. This 128 km natural path, built on an old railway line, offers a scenic route through olive groves and over impressive metallic viaducts, making it a worthwhile excursion for cyclists staying in Alcalá La Real.
tips: Also known as the castle of Alcalá la Real, the fortress of La Mota is a defensive enclosure, located on the hill of La Mota at 1029m, in the city of Alcalá la Real, in the province of Jaén. It is dated from the Nasrid period (13th and 14th centuries), although with some previous elements.

Fountain located in the homonymous square in Alcalá la Real, Jaén.
The legend of the Moorish is a legend from Jaén that tells how a young woman of incomparable beauty finds herself in need of going down to a Christian spring for water for her sick mother. In the middle of the way, she was surprised by a captain, the young Moor explained that she needed to fill the pitcher with water. The captain – who fell in love with her – told her that to fulfill her wish she had to remove her veil to contemplate her beauty.

You can read the whole legend here: https://www.jaen24h.com/actualidad/leyendas-de-jaen-la-leyenda-de-la-mora-de-alcala-la-real
Small Catholic sanctuary located in the town of Charilla. Charilla, is a village in Alcalá la Real, Jaén, from which it is 6 kilometers away. In Roman times it was known as Flora.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is approximately 24.2 km long and is estimated to take around 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling time and does not include stops for sightseeing or rest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, but it involves significant climbing. The area has an irregular topography, and you should expect substantial elevation gains. The climb to the Fortaleza de la Mota is particularly steep, with sections reaching an average of 10% gradient over 900 meters, and the final approach within the fortress includes cobblestones. The area around Charilla also features very challenging uphill sections, with 'tremendous ramps' and a 'wall' of 1.8 kilometers at a 13.9% gradient, with maximums up to 21%. While many surfaces are paved, some rural segments might be unpaved.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Fortaleza de la Mota y Charilla circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of historical and natural sights. You'll visit the historic Fortaleza de la Mota, a Nasrid-era castle with panoramic views. Other highlights include the Fuente de la Mora and the Church of Our Lady of the Rosary of Charilla. The landscape features vast olive groves, remnants of Mediterranean forest, impressive sharp-cut rocks, and traditional rural villages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Sierra Sur de Jaén region, including this route, is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making the significant climbs more manageable and the scenery more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cold with potential for rain.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car for this cycling route in Alcalá la Real?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route aren't detailed, Alcalá la Real, as the starting and ending point, will have public parking options. It's advisable to look for parking within the town center or near the base of the climb to the Fortaleza de la Mota.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the starting point in Alcalá la Real?
faqs.answer: Alcalá la Real is a municipality in Jaén province, Andalusia, and is generally accessible by intercity bus services from larger cities in the region. However, specific public transport options that accommodate bicycles directly to the trailhead might be limited. It's best to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport in advance.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits are required for cycling on public roads and trails in the Jaén region. However, there might be an entrance fee to access the interior of the Fortaleza de la Mota itself if you choose to explore it. Always check the fortress's official website for current admission details.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on public roads and trails, specific rules may apply to certain historical sites or natural parks. If you plan to visit the interior of the Fortaleza de la Mota, it's best to check their pet policy beforehand. Always keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Andalucía - North Route (Província de Jaén), the Camino Mozárabe, and Los Zumaques for portions of your ride.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants available along the route or in Alcalá la Real?
faqs.answer: Alcalá la Real, as a town, offers various cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options suitable for cyclists. While the route passes through rural areas and the village of Charilla, it's advisable to stock up on water and snacks in Alcalá la Real before starting, especially given the challenging climbs. You'll find more amenities in the main town.

faqs.question: What is an insider tip for tackling the challenging climbs on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the significant and steep climbs, particularly to the Fortaleza de la Mota and around Charilla, it's highly recommended to pace yourself and save energy for these sections. Consider starting early in the morning to avoid the midday heat, especially outside of peak seasons. Ensure your bicycle is in excellent condition with appropriate gearing for steep ascents.
